latest.title: Second Price Monitoring Extn
latest.content:
9 Mar 2021 14:05
RNS Number : 6770R
Shefa Gems Ltd
09 March 2021
Second Price Monitoring Extension
A second and final Price Monitoring Extension has been activated in this security. The auction call period is extended in this security for a further 5 minutes.
Following the first price monitoring extension this security would still have executed more than a pre-determined percentage above or below the price of the most recent automated execution today. London Stock Exchange electronic order book users have a final opportunity to review the prices and sizes of orders entered in this security prior to the auction execution.
The applicable percentage is set by reference to a security's Millennium Exchange sector. This is set out in the Sector Breakdown tab of the Parameters document at
www.londonstockexchange.com/tradingservices
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Price Monitoring Extension
latest.content:
9 Mar 2021 14:00
RNS Number : 6754R
Shefa Gems Ltd
09 March 2021
Price Monitoring Extension
The auction call period has been extended in this security by 5 minutes.
Auction call extensions give London Stock Exchange electronic order book users a further opportunity to review the prices and sizes of orders entered in an individual security's auction call before the execution occurs. A price monitoring extension is activated when the matching process would have otherwise resulted in an execution price that is a pre-determined percentage above or below the price of the most recent automated execution today.
The applicable percentage is set by reference to a security's Millennium Exchange sector. This is set out in the Sector Breakdown tab of the Parameters document at
www.londonstockexchange.com/tradingservices
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: SEFA is a blockchain‑focused investment platform that raises capital for tech ventures and manages a portfolio of SPAC‑backed projects.
company_30s.why_they_matter: It gives investors a way to tap into emerging blockchain infrastructure and tokenization, competing with larger crypto funds and providing liquidity to niche projects.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in its early growth phase with limited revenue streams and heavy cash burn. Recent filings show ongoing financial reporting delays, signalling operational challenges. The share price sits at a very low level, reflecting market scepticism.

rns_narrative: The company is busy tightening its governance – board changes, new circulars and a proposed combined transaction are all on the agenda.
Financial reporting remains fragmented – delayed audited results, interim results and dividend announcements point to cash‑flow stress.
There is a faint hint of activity around a potential SPAC merger, but nothing concrete has been confirmed yet.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet grind of a company battling survival
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None – the few bullish voices are tied to speculation about a SPAC merge.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Investors worry about persistent losses, dilution from funding rounds and regulatory scrutiny over SPAC operations.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has stayed flat; no new enthusiasm has emerged, and the chatter remains minimal.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat, indicating no public interest spikes.

near_term_catalysts: A strategic partnership or a SPAC merger announcement that could inject fresh capital.
A clear earnings beat or a dividend payout that signals financial health.
Resolution of the audit delay and publication of audited results.
A board appointment that brings experienced leadership to the helm.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a surge in trading volume (10× average), a price break above the recent 0.8p resistance, or a positive RNS that lifts confidence.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery from a severe sell‑off. The tight price compression suggests a breakout could signal the start of mean reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share has fallen dramatically – a steep drop creates a huge upside if it rebounds to its 4p peak. Historically, deep crashes have delivered returns over 100%. A SPAC merge or new partnership could trigger a rapid rally.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company is still grappling with cash‑flow issues and regulatory delays. Failure to secure additional funding or a regulatory setback could keep the price flat or push it lower.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of 6‑7 times the current price versus a downside of up to 90% if the company fails to reverse. The asymmetry favours a cautious long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 0.6p support, size a small position, and set a stop 0.2p below support. Allocate 2% of capital per trade and aim to exit near 2.5p if a breakout occurs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until volume exceeds 10× the average and the price breaks above 0.8p. Also monitor for a positive RNS or a SPAC announcement.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks identified.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A steep plunge into a quiet market offers a 6‑fold upside if a SPAC merge or new partnership materialises, but the risk of continued decline remains high.
